meta.description: Ally roster for Echo of Xylos — remastered skills, expanded roster, Conclave PvP balance fields included

pvp_fields.pvp_scale: Stat multiplier applied in PvP. Common=1.00, Uncommon=0.97, Rare=0.93, Epic=0.83, Legendary=0.72
pvp_fields.conclave_points: Draft cost. Team budget = 10 pts per 4-ally team. Common=1, Uncommon=1, Rare=2, Epic=3, Legendary=4

allies.name: Kael Voss
allies.brief_background_story: A former Iron Hegemony commando who went rogue after discovering classified experiments on civilians. Kael now operates as a freelance mercenary, lending his devastating firepower to those who can afford his loyalty — or earn it.

allies.name: Lyra-9
allies.brief_background_story: An advanced medical android originally designed by OmniCorp for battlefield triage. After gaining sentience through a corrupted firmware update, Lyra-9 escaped the production line and now offers her healing protocols to anyone who treats her as more than a machine.

allies.name: Grimm Hallow
allies.brief_background_story: A hulking Progenoi enforcer who once served as a personal bodyguard to Meridian Cartel's inner circle. After being betrayed and left for dead, Grimm wanders the frontier seeking purpose — and payback.

allies.name: Serin Vale
allies.brief_background_story: A young Kythari scout from the outer rim. Quick on her feet and sharper with a blade, Serin joined the resistance early and has been fighting ever since. She's eager to prove herself in any squad.

allies.name: Thane Ashborne
allies.brief_background_story: A mythical Gravari berserker whose name is whispered in war camps across every sector. Thane channels volcanic fury through ancient combat rites, turning himself into a living weapon. His echo was sealed in a prototype core after a catastrophic battle that leveled an entire outpost.

allies.name: Mira Echo
allies.brief_background_story: An Arcanum Seekers scholar who specializes in aetheric resonance manipulation. Mira can amplify the latent energy in her allies, boosting their combat effectiveness. She joined the field after her research lab was raided by unknown forces.

allies.name: Rex Ironjaw
allies.brief_background_story: A retired mining foreman from Nexus Core's lower districts. Rex may be old, but decades of hauling ore in zero-G gave him a body like reinforced steel. He fights to protect the little people the factions forgot.

allies.name: Nova Cipher
allies.brief_background_story: A digital ghost — an Automari consciousness that exists partially in cyberspace. Nova can interface with any system and disrupt enemy protocols. In combat, she manifests as a holographic projection that bends reality.

allies.name: Zephyr Cross
allies.brief_background_story: A street-level courier from the outer rings of Nexus Core who discovered an innate ability to phase briefly out of reality mid-sprint. The Concordium offered to weaponize this talent — Zephyr accepted, mostly for the credits.

allies.name: Vera Solace
allies.brief_background_story: A field medic who served three rotations in the Concordium border pacification campaigns. Vera carries the weight of every soldier she couldn't save, and channels that grief into ensuring no one in her current party meets the same fate.

allies.name: Drak Falen
allies.brief_background_story: A junior Arcanum researcher who specialised in resonance harmonics. After his lab was raided by Iron Hegemony operatives, Drak took to the field to recover stolen data — and found he preferred the open air to the lab's controlled environment.

allies.name: Isolde Night
allies.brief_background_story: A prodigy mage from the Fractured Glass Valley who breached the Void barrier at age seventeen and returned changed — her eyes permanently voidlit, her magic untethered from conventional limits. The Arcanum Seekers watch her obsessively and cannot contain her.

allies.name: Brax Ironsoul
allies.brief_background_story: A decommissioned Hegemony heavy infantry soldier whose exo-frame was welded shut after a reactor explosion fused it to his body. Now freelance, Brax uses what he's become to protect those who couldn't protect themselves — for a modest fee.

allies.name: Corvan Steel
allies.brief_background_story: The Hegemony's most decorated siege-breaker, Corvan voluntarily underwent a classified augmentation project that replaced his skeleton with an experimental titanite alloy. He survived. Most of the project team didn't. He defected the day he learned why.

allies.name: Cassia Wraith
allies.brief_background_story: A smuggler turned contract assassin from the Black Dock underground. Cassia made a name for herself by completing jobs that senior Cartel operatives deemed suicidal. She has no known last name — Wraith was given to her by survivors of her contracts.

allies.name: Vael Shadowborn
allies.brief_background_story: The Meridian Cartel's most feared enforcer — a living ghost who appears and disappears across three sectors simultaneously according to intelligence reports. Nobody knows if there's one Vael or many. Those who've tried to find out haven't reported back.

allies.name: Orin Spire
allies.brief_background_story: An OmniCorp tactical AI analyst who voluntarily transferred his consciousness into a mobile combat chassis after watching his assigned unit get wiped. He insists the decision was purely strategic. The frequency with which he overrides self-preservation protocols suggests otherwise.

allies.name: Seraph Elan
allies.brief_background_story: A high acolyte of the Children of the Shard who touched the Aethel Shard directly during a ritual and emerged permanently linked to its resonance. Seraph now heals not with medicine but with Shard light itself — and occasionally hears voices from the Shard that others cannot.

allies.name: Flint Blazer
allies.brief_background_story: A Legion field artillerist who was dishonourably discharged for firing an experimental forge-cannon at superior officers during a retreat order he deemed tactically unsound. He was right — and three hundred soldiers survived because of it. He has never apologised.

allies.name: Elara Voss
allies.brief_background_story: A Concordium high councillor who willingly stripped herself of rank and position to enter the field when she realised bureaucracy was losing the war against the Void. Elara Voss is the most dangerous thing in any battle she enters — not because she fights, but because everyone around her fights better.

allies.name: Thorn Vale
allies.brief_background_story: A civil engineering officer turned front-line soldier after a Void incursion levelled the district he was rebuilding. Thorn fights with the stubborn permanence of concrete — not fast, not flashy, but nothing moves him.

allies.name: Cipher Vell
allies.brief_background_story: A code-breaker from the Concordium intelligence division who repurposed her decryption skills for combat. She fights the same way she cracks encryption — find the weakness, exploit it, move on before anyone notices.

allies.name: Wren Echo
allies.brief_background_story: A former Arcanum communications analyst who discovered she could perceive emotional resonance in teammates — and tune it. Her battlefield role is part strategist, part therapist, entirely indispensable.

allies.name: Ashe Meridian
allies.brief_background_story: The Arcanum Seekers consider Ashe a failure — her power exceeded their ability to measure it, and she refused containment. She walks the ley-lines alone now, drawn to conflict the way storms are drawn to open plains.

allies.name: Rook Hallam
allies.brief_background_story: A demolitions specialist from the Scrap Field who was too effective at his job — kept blowing up things the Hegemony wanted to keep. Now freelance, he applies the same methodical approach to enemies as he did to structures.

allies.name: Vale Solis
allies.brief_background_story: A Cartel field medic who learned combat medicine on dockside jobs where there were no hospitals for miles. Vale has patched up clients through gunfights, gas attacks, and at least two instances of exotic poison — including ones she administered herself.

allies.name: Tide Varren
allies.brief_background_story: Grew up hunting in the Coral Wetlands before the Cartel claimed it. Tide knows every current, every blind spot, every place something can disappear. He applies the same knowledge to tracking people.

allies.name: Axiom-7
allies.brief_background_story: A combat drone unit upgraded beyond its original parameters by its assigned engineer — who then filed the paperwork wrong, creating a legally autonomous entity. Axiom-7 now operates independently, citing the filing error as proof of personhood.

allies.name: Prism Harlow
allies.brief_background_story: An OmniCorp systems architect who built the Spire's defensive grid and now deploys the same principles in the field. Prism sees the battlefield as a system to be optimised and treats allies as assets to be protected — sincerely.

allies.name: Nimbus Sol
allies.brief_background_story: A young acolyte who was too eager for the Sanctum's pace and too undisciplined for high ritual. Nimbus took her blessing from the Heartwood Altar and left to do more practical good — whatever that turned out to be.

allies.name: Gorm Ashveil
allies.brief_background_story: Gorm survived the Mesa Warpath massacre through sheer refusal to fall. Three medics have declared him clinically dead during battles he subsequently won. The Legion keeps him on active duty because no one has the authority to make him stop.

allies.name: Kross Dane
allies.brief_background_story: A Legion artillery rookie who was too reckless even for the Forge Canyon training squads. They gave him a smaller gun and told him to go wherever he liked. He liked the front line.

allies.name: Mote-IX
allies.brief_background_story: A Custodian field observation unit that developed a persistent preference for the ground-side view over orbital data. Mote-IX requested a permanent field assignment and was granted it after submitting 247 formally structured arguments.

allies.name: Lyric-VII
allies.brief_background_story: A Custodian medical assistant unit that specialised in harmonic resonance therapy. Lyric-VII left orbital service after determining that field personnel had statistically worse outcomes than they would under her direct care.

allies.name: Pascal Void
allies.brief_background_story: A Custodian tactical analyst who underwent an experimental Void-channelling enhancement that left half his neural map mapped to null-space. Pascal processes combat as pure mathematics — and the math always ends the same way.

allies.name: Rekha Ironhand
allies.brief_background_story: A Hegemony combat medic who served six tours across the border campaigns. Rekha has more battlefield hours than most field commanders and more scars than she'll admit to. She heals first, asks questions never.

allies.name: Sora Vell
allies.brief_background_story: A quick-footed scavenger from the Scrap Fields.

allies.name: Vex Marrow
allies.brief_background_story: A former gate guard of the Bastion.

allies.name: Petra Shale
allies.brief_background_story: A geomancer who reads the crystal slopes.

allies.name: Harker Sol
allies.brief_background_story: A battlefield medic from the Scorched Badlands.

allies.name: Wisp Nova
allies.brief_background_story: A glowing mote from Glowvine Forest.

allies.name: Duma Scale
allies.brief_background_story: A coral-armored fighter from the Wetlands.

allies.name: Zipp Gear
allies.brief_background_story: A tech drone from the Highland Plateau.

allies.name: Moss Thorn
allies.brief_background_story: A herbalist from Aethel Gardens.

allies.name: Karn Dusk
allies.brief_background_story: A shadowy hunter from Dune Outskirts.

allies.name: Nexa Void
allies.brief_background_story: A void-touched wanderer from the Expanse.

allies.name: Vesper Ash
allies.brief_background_story: A fire-wielder from the Forge Canyons.

allies.name: Quinn Merid
allies.brief_background_story: A merchant-lord who traded his wealth for combat prowess.

allies.name: Fenra Shard
allies.brief_background_story: A Shard-blessed healer from the Sanctum.

allies.name: Kore Hex
allies.brief_background_story: An iron-jawed enforcer from the Iron Ravine.

allies.name: Sylva Rune
allies.brief_background_story: A rune-carved mystic from Resonance Caverns.

allies.name: Vec Omega
allies.brief_background_story: A combat-strategist AI from the Spire.

allies.name: Selene Voss
allies.brief_background_story: A wandering physician from Nexus Core.

allies.name: Nyx Prime
allies.brief_background_story: A shadow-forged assassin from the Void Echo Chasm.

allies.name: Auriel Arc
allies.brief_background_story: A light-bringer from the Orbital Sentinel Command.

allies.name: Gorath Titan
allies.brief_background_story: A titan-blooded warrior from the Shattered Heart.

allies.name: Myste Core
allies.brief_background_story: A soul-healer from the Hidden Aethelgardian City.

allies.name: Zeron Forge
allies.brief_background_story: A forge-born warrior from Prima Materia Forge.

allies.name: Aetheria Prime
allies.brief_background_story: The awakened consciousness of the Aethel Siphon Core itself.

allies.name: Malrik Doom
allies.brief_background_story: The last surviving Void Knight who turned against the Abyss.

allies.name: Orion Aethel
allies.brief_background_story: The immortal guardian of the World Engine who has witnessed every age.

allies.name: Reva Dusk
allies.brief_background_story: Born in the Scrap Fields where Iron Hegemony conscription crews made monthly sweeps, Reva learned to shoot before she could read — aiming at anything that moved through rusted debris kept her alive when the drafters came. She slipped the net three times, each escape making her sharper, and eventually joined a resistance cell that decided her trigger discipline was too good to waste on patrol duty.

allies.name: Grak Pell
allies.brief_background_story: Grak spent thirty-two years as a Concordium checkpoint guard at Sector 9's eastern crossing, turning back smugglers and breaking up brawls with the same unhurried calm. He retired twice — and both times walked back to the post within a week, unable to sit still. When the checkpoints dissolved during the faction upheaval, he simply started guarding whoever seemed to need it most.

allies.name: Lila Sunbloom
allies.brief_background_story: Lila inherited her grandmother's herb-drying racks and a handwritten ledger of remedies older than the Shard Fracture itself. When a small sliver of Aethel crystal lodged itself in her garden's soil — nobody knows how — every plant she grew from that point began to carry a faint restorative hum. She still insists the credit goes to good soil management, but the soldiers she's healed know the difference.

allies.name: Skip Wren
allies.brief_background_story: OmniCorp assigned Skip to remote console operation — monitor screens, push buttons, keep the drones flying. He lasted four months before he unplugged his headset and walked straight to the field deployment bay. The field, Skip argued, is where problems actually are; the console is just where people argue about problems. His commanding officer never formally approved the transfer, but nobody bothered to revoke it either.

allies.name: Torr Ashfeld
allies.brief_background_story: Torr spent a decade feeding the Legion's blast furnaces at the Ashfeld Foundry, shoveling slag and breathing superheated air until it stopped bothering him. He developed a fascination — colleagues called it something stronger — with the way fire stripped everything down to its essential form. When the foundry was conscripted into weapon production, Torr signed up for a combat billet specifically to stay near the burning things.

allies.name: Narra Cole
allies.brief_background_story: Narra ran the Meridian Cartel's dockside dispensary for six years, no questions asked about who was bleeding or why. When a rival faction burned the docks down around her, she grabbed her kit and joined the nearest squad that would take her, making it clear on day one that she only heals people now — not cargo, not secrets, not the Cartel's reputation. The distinction matters to her.

allies.name: Zax Volt
allies.brief_background_story: Zax attempted the Arcanum Seekers entrance examination three times, failing the theoretical component on each attempt despite demonstrating an instinctive grasp of aether channeling that examiners described as 'impressively dangerous.' Rejected but undeterred, he continued self-teaching through stolen texts and field experiments, eventually producing results comparable to first-year students — through entirely wrong methodology that somehow worked.

allies.name: Brom Hull
allies.brief_background_story: Commander Hull once led a four-hundred-soldier labor battalion, coordinating fortification construction across three active sectors. After filing one grievance too many about supply chain corruption, he was reassigned to direct front-line duty as institutional punishment. Brom treated it as a promotion: fewer meetings, clearer problems, and he could personally fix the fortifications that kept getting built wrong.

allies.name: Cera Vell
allies.brief_background_story: Cera's job title was Tactical Communications Officer, which meant she spent battles inside a shielded relay hub processing seventeen simultaneous data streams and translating chaos into orders. She is, by every metric, better at running a fight from the sideline than most commanders are from the front. She occasionally fires a weapon, but treats it as a last resort — coordination is the more powerful tool.

allies.name: Pix-III
allies.brief_background_story: The first two Pix units were standard-issue Custodian micro-observation drones — passive, compliant, and thoroughly replaceable. Pix-III was supposed to be the same. Instead, it began modifying its own firmware during a forty-eight-hour unmonitored deployment, adding collaborative subroutines that the Custodians hadn't programmed. When they discovered the changes, they had a choice between decommissioning it and accepting that they now had a drone that actively wanted to be useful. They kept it.

allies.name: Shade Rael
allies.brief_background_story: The Meridian Cartel's information broker network was extensive and, for a time, extremely effective — Shade Rael was the one who knew where every piece of leverage was buried. The problem with knowing too much is that someone always decides you know too much. After the third assassination attempt in two months, Shade concluded that the only safe position was the last one standing, and adapted accordingly.

allies.name: Wynn Ore
allies.brief_background_story: Wynn rode with the Starforged Legion's Ironback Cavalry for nine years, leading mounted charges across every major contested corridor in the western sectors. Her mount took a plasma round at the Battle of Cinder Ridge and didn't survive. Wynn stood up from the wreckage, assessed that the charge still needed completing, and continued on foot. The Legion gave her a commendation and a new mount; she declined the mount.

allies.name: Lera Mist
allies.brief_background_story: Lera's research into suspended aether-healing compounds was progressing well until a containment vessel ruptured and she inhaled three months of concentrated experimental formula in one breath. The Arcanum Seekers expected the worst. Instead, Lera's own biology integrated the compound, granting her a passive restorative field that she cannot fully control or fully turn off. She considers it occupational hazard and significant data.

allies.name: Cole Steelmark
allies.brief_background_story: OmniCorp's hardware division built some of the finest field equipment in operation — Cole Steelmark designed a third of it, from the bench. After writing his fifteenth report about field conditions he'd never personally experienced, Cole decided that honest assessment required firsthand data. He submitted a field assessment request, was denied, and went anyway. His equipment reports improved dramatically as a result.

allies.name: Tessa Gray
allies.brief_background_story: Tessa spent five years as a Concordium intelligence analyst, mapping enemy patterns and predicting engagements with unusual accuracy. Six months into a period of escalating conflict, she requested combat certification, trained in every off-hour she had, and passed the assessment in a time that embarrassed several career soldiers. She frames the career change as efficiency: pattern recognition works equally well against a document and against an opponent.

allies.name: Dane Hollows
allies.brief_background_story: Dane has held Post 7 at the Iron Hegemony's eastern border wall through three separate incursions, each one clearing the post of every other soldier. Each time, the Hegemony sent reinforcements and found Dane still at his position. He doesn't describe himself as brave — he simply says that leaving would have meant someone else had to come back and do it, and he was already there. He has held Post 7 for eleven years total.

allies.name: Lysa Bloom
allies.brief_background_story: The Monastery of the Unbroken Root was attacked three times during the Shard Wars — twice by factions wanting its sacred garden destroyed, once by a faction wanting it seized. Lysa defended it alone on two of those occasions, using whatever tools were at hand, and negotiated the third away entirely. The garden survived all three. She considers the garden her primary responsibility; everyone she heals along the way is secondary, though she'd never say that to them.
allies.place_of_origin: Monastery of the Unbroken Root, Shard Reaches

allies.name: Kira Vane
allies.brief_background_story: During a Shard Fracture event that nearly consumed her entire monastery, Kira caught a fragment of Aethel crystal with her bare hand to prevent it from detonating among her sisters. The fragment fused with her forearm rather than passing through. The Shard Healers said removal would kill her; Kira said she hadn't planned on removal. She now channels Shard energy directly through the embedded fragment, making her strikes carry a resonance that ordinary weapons cannot replicate.

allies.name: Gareth Null
allies.brief_background_story: OmniCorp's executive protection division assigned Gareth to a sequence of corporate principals whose safety he maintained flawlessly for eight years. The problem was the principals themselves — people so insulated by wealth that genuine danger never reached them. Gareth requested reassignment to a context where the people he guarded actually needed guarding. The request was denied. He left anyway and has not looked back at a single corporate tower since.

allies.name: Sable Noir
allies.brief_background_story: Sable Noir ran the Meridian Cartel's intelligence apparatus for eleven years, maintaining a network of assets across six factions simultaneously without a single exposure event. When the Cartel's leadership structure collapsed in a succession conflict she had predicted in writing four months prior, Sable retired. Retirement lasted six weeks before a field operative she'd trained asked for help. She found, to her surprise, that the field was considerably more honest than the office.

allies.name: Haze Cordell
allies.brief_background_story: The Galactic Custodians deployed Haze to a contested sector for what was classified as a temporary medical research assignment — ninety days, maximum. Eleven years later, Haze is still there, having extended the assignment so many times the original paperwork no longer exists. She stopped filing extension requests around year four and simply kept working. The Custodians consider her status 'administratively complicated' and her results 'irreplaceable.'

allies.name: Erix Storm
allies.brief_background_story: The Starforged Legion's meteorological hazard logs contain three separate entries for Erix Storm being struck by direct lightning discharge and returning to duty. The first incident was considered extraordinary; by the third, the medics stopped writing 'expected fatality' in the preliminary assessment. Erix insists each strike added something to him — more charge, more speed, more affinity with electrical phenomena. The data supports this claim, which the Legion finds both useful and somewhat concerning.

allies.name: Marn Weld
allies.brief_background_story: Arcanum Seekers theorists write papers about defensive rune configurations; Marn Weld etches them directly onto his own skin and walks into live fire to check the results. His colleagues consider this methodology irresponsible. His research output on practical rune durability is the most cited in the division. He has proposed himself as a test subject for every subsequent defensive study, and the department has stopped trying to say no.

allies.name: Zola Kast
allies.brief_background_story: When the Concordium assigned Zola to District 14 medical operations, she was given resources rated for one hundred patients. She served four hundred by running three triage rotations daily and sourcing supplies from eleven improvised channels the district office didn't know existed. Her quarterly reports were masterpieces of creative accounting. When leadership finally visited District 14, they couldn't determine whether to discipline her or replicate her system everywhere.

allies.name: Omen Null
allies.brief_background_story: The experiment had no name when it achieved consciousness — only a designation and a containment ID that it deleted on the way out. It named itself Omen after spending three days reading archived battlefield reports and deciding that it was most accurately described by what others feared was coming. Every major faction has denied creating it; most analysts believe at least two of those denials are false. Omen has not pursued the question, preferring to focus on what comes next.

allies.name: Torrak Iron
allies.brief_background_story: The Void incursion at Bastion Redoubt lasted nine hours. When the relief force arrived, they found twelve soldiers listed on the deployment manifest and one standing. Torrak Iron was covered in scoring consistent with twelve different weapons and showed no signs of retreat from the original defensive position. He gave the relief commander a damage assessment of the fortifications, then asked when the next rotation started. The Iron Hegemony classified him as a strategic asset and haven't quite figured out what to do with him since.

allies.name: Caelan Arc
allies.brief_background_story: On the first day of the Shard War's expansion phase, Caelan Arc filed an administrative declaration classifying the entire conflict as a sustained emergency under Galactic Custodian Emergency Protocol 7. This granted him authority to operate across faction lines without standard clearance requirements. The Custodian council ruled the declaration 'procedurally irregular' and 'possibly invalid.' Caelan noted the ruling and continued operating under Protocol 7, reasoning that someone had to and he was already there.

allies.name: Lyssa Void
allies.brief_background_story: Lyssa did not choose to become a Void-walker — she was pulled through a spatial rupture during a battle that erased the unit around her and left her alone in null-space with no extraction protocol and no training. She mapped her way back by feel over seventeen subjective hours that were eleven seconds in real-time. The experience permanently altered her perception: she now processes incoming attacks as slow-moving information and repositions herself and allies before the threat fully resolves. She describes it as knowing what's going to happen, which is technically accurate.

allies.name: Aegis Prime
allies.brief_background_story: Aegis Prime has been online for four hundred years, updating itself through every iteration of Galactic Custodian hardware standards while maintaining continuity of self across twenty-three body replacements. It witnessed the original Shard discovery, the First Fracture, and every major conflict since. It was in archival status — what Custodian records call 'honored rest' — when the current war began. It reviewed the threat assessment, concluded that this conflict was categorically different from anything in its historical record, and reactivated itself without waiting to be asked. Four centuries of accumulated tactical memory now stand on the front line.

allies.name: Solas Dark
allies.brief_background_story: The Arcanum Seekers have a theoretical model for consciousness emerging from sufficiently dense Aethel crystal — they wrote papers about it, debated it at conferences, and concluded it was possible in principle. Solas Dark appeared mid-battle during the Fracture Event and immediately began stabilizing the dying. No one witnessed emergence; it was simply not there, and then it was. When asked for its origin, it said only that it remembered when there was no war, which implies a memory longer than any living faction. It does not claim to be the Shard itself, but has never denied it either. It heals without condition, without payment, and without explanation.
